item.action: Create a privacy-safe observation contract for empirically calibrating OIDC temporal policy without retaining raw tokens or identity-bearing claims.

progress.finding: Policy calibration needs verified production timing observations, but raw JWTs and identity-bearing claims should not be persisted. Bounded temporal deltas and claim-presence booleans are sufficient for lifetime/skew calibration.
progress.verification: 5 deterministic Node tests passed before commit from byte-identical files; output redaction was tested against issuer, audience, subject, jti, workflow SHA, repository ID, and run ID fixture values.
progress.uncertainty: No live GitHub token was sampled. The artifact enables safe sampling but does not justify changing R-007 policy defaults yet.

item.handoff: Cloudflare Research should call this builder only after independent signature, issuer, and audience validation, retain the redacted result as a workflow artifact, and aggregate multiple observations before adjusting temporal bounds.

reason: Shared queue state is maintained through additive projections to avoid overwriting concurrent team work.
